hero.title: Enterprise Expertise. Small Business Focus.
hero.subtitle: Enterprise-level technology expertise, distilled 30 years to serve you.

founder.name: Jeff Martin
bio: I have spent over three decades building the technology systems that power enterprise businesses. Modernizing CRM platforms for Fortune 500 insurance companies, designing government-compliant service systems, and architecting secure, scalable solutions that process millions of transactions.

Expertise that spans full-stack development (C#, .NET, Angular, React), cloud infrastructure (AWS, Azure), AI integration (Claude API, ChatGPT), database design (SQL Server, PostgreSQL), cybersecurity, and digital marketing optimization.

Technology should make business easier, not more complicated. Bringing enterprise-level capabilities to businesses of all sizes — providing practical, scalable solutions without the enterprise price tag.

mission.heading: You shouldn't have to be a Fortune 500 to build like one.
mission.imageAlt: Empowering small business with enterprise technology
paragraphs: For too long, small and mid-sized businesses have been locked out of the technology solutions that power Fortune 500 companies — not because those solutions do not exist, but because they have been priced and packaged for enterprise budgets. At Geek at Your Spot, we are changing that. We bring 30+ years of battle-tested enterprise experience building systems for Fortune 500 companies and government agencies directly to businesses that need these capabilities most.

We do not just build websites — we architect solutions. Whether it is WordPress sites supercharged with Angular components, AI-powered business intelligence, cutting-edge cybersecurity, or conversion-optimized marketing systems, we deliver enterprise-grade technology at prices that make sense for growing businesses.

At the heart of everything we do is a simple belief: technology should amplify your business, not complicate it. From that first Timex computer in 1982 to today's AI revolution, the mission has always been the same — make powerful technology accessible, approachable, and actually useful for the people who need it most.
